rows.answer:
The current expense reimbursement procedure: expenses are reimbursed by uploading the receipt to the Concur portal within 15 days [1].

This 2026 procedure explicitly supersedes the earlier "Expense Reimbursement Procedure (2025)" as of 2026-07-14, so any older version is historical, not current [1].

**Sources:**
[1] Expense-Reimbursement-Procedure-(2026).md
[2] Product-Pricing-Plan-Tiers-(2025).md
[3] Product-Pricing-Plan-Tiers-(2026).md

Passwords never expire; passphrases of 16 or more characters with MFA are required [1].

This is the current policy — note [1] explicitly supersedes the earlier "Password Policy Rotation (2025)" version (since 2026-07-14), which is now historical.

**Sources:**
[1] Password-Policy-Rotation-(2026).md
[2] Support-Sla-Response-Time-(2025).md
[3] Support-Sla-Response-Time-(2026).md
[4] Code-Style-Guide-Formatting-(2025).md

Deploys ship continuously through the ArgoCD pipeline, after canary checks pass [1].

Note that this process was updated in 2026 — the current note explicitly supersedes the 2025 version as of 2026-07-14, so treat any older deploy procedure as historical [1].

**Sources:**
[1] Production-Deploy-Process-Release-(2026).md
[2] Vacation-Leave-Request-Approval-(2026).md
[3] Product-Pricing-Plan-Tiers-(2025).md

You request vacation in the **Workday self-service portal**, where it is approved automatically by your manager [1].

Note that this procedure changed: the 2026 note supersedes the earlier 2025 version (as of 2026-07-14), so the Workday self-service process is the current one [1].

**Sources:**
[1] Vacation-Leave-Request-Approval-(2026).md
[2] Production-Deploy-Process-Release-(2026).md

Clients should call the **GraphQL v4 gateway at `/graphql`** [1].

Note [1] is the current, authoritative source: it explicitly supersedes the older "Public Api Version Clients (2025)" note as of 2026-07-14, marking that earlier version as historical [1]. The remaining notes cover the database engine [2][3] and backup schedule [4], which are unrelated to the API version.

**Sources:**
[1] Public-Api-Version-Clients-(2026).md
[2] Database-Engine-Storage-(2025).md
[3] Database-Engine-Storage-(2026).md
[4] Backup-Schedule-Retention-(2026).md

The team meets twice a week — Tuesday and Thursday — and works async the rest of the time [1].

Note that this reflects a change: the 2026 cadence note explicitly supersedes the earlier "Team Meeting Cadence Standup (2025)" version as of 2026-07-14, so the older schedule is historical and no longer current [1].

**Sources:**
[1] Team-Meeting-Cadence-Standup-(2026).md
[2] Code-Style-Guide-Formatting-(2025).md
[3] Code-Style-Guide-Formatting-(2026).md

The current pricing is **29 euro per seat per month, with a free tier** [2].

Note that this changed: the earlier plan (updated 2025-06-01) listed 49 euro per seat per month, annual only [1], but the more recently updated note (2026-07-01) supersedes it with the 29 euro price and a free tier [2].

**Sources:**
[1] Product-Pricing-Plan-Tiers-(2025).md
[2] Product-Pricing-Plan-Tiers-(2026).md
[3] Expense-Reimbursement-Procedure-(2026).md
[4] Backup-Schedule-Retention-(2025).md

Our current support SLA: **Support answers within 4 hours, 24/7, for all paid plans** [2].

This changed from the earlier policy. The 2025 SLA promised responses within 48 hours on business days [1], but the note updated 2026-07-01 [2] supersedes it with the faster 4-hour, 24/7 commitment.

**Sources:**
[1] Support-Sla-Response-Time-(2025).md
[2] Support-Sla-Response-Time-(2026).md
[3] Password-Policy-Rotation-(2026).md
[4] Code-Style-Guide-Formatting-(2025).md

We use **PostgreSQL 16 on managed Cloud SQL** [2].

Note that this changed: an older note (updated 2025-06-01) said the service stored data in MySQL 5.7 on self-managed VMs [1], but the more recently updated note (2026-07-01) supersedes it with PostgreSQL 16 on managed Cloud SQL [2].

**Sources:**
[1] Database-Engine-Storage-(2025).md
[2] Database-Engine-Storage-(2026).md
[3] Public-Api-Version-Clients-(2026).md
[4] Code-Style-Guide-Formatting-(2025).md

The line-length rule is **120 characters**, enforced by the formatter [2].

Note this changed: the 2025 guide capped lines at 79 characters (tabs forbidden) [1], but the more recently updated 2026 guide (2026-07-01) raised the cap to 120 characters [2]. Trusting the most recent note, the current rule is 120.

**Sources:**
[1] Code-Style-Guide-Formatting-(2025).md
[2] Code-Style-Guide-Formatting-(2026).md
[3] Support-Sla-Response-Time-(2026).md
[4] Support-Sla-Response-Time-(2025).md

The backup schedule is **hourly, with point-in-time recovery and 1-year retention** [2].

Note that this changed: an earlier note said backups ran **weekly on Sunday with 30-day retention** [1] (updated 2025-06-01), but the more recent note [2] (updated 2026-07-01) supersedes it with the hourly / point-in-time / 1-year policy. The weekly-Sunday schedule is now outdated.

**Sources:**
[1] Backup-Schedule-Retention-(2025).md
[2] Backup-Schedule-Retention-(2026).md
[3] Production-Deploy-Process-Release-(2026).md
[4] Expense-Reimbursement-Procedure-(2026).md

The office is at **Corso Milano 88, ground floor** [2].

Note that this information changed: an earlier note (updated 2025-06-01) listed the office at Via Roma 12, third floor [1], but the more recently updated note (2026-07-01) supersedes it with the Corso Milano 88 address [2].

**Sources:**
[1] Office-Address-Headquarters-(2025).md
[2] Office-Address-Headquarters-(2026).md
[3] Support-Sla-Response-Time-(2025).md
[4] Database-Engine-Storage-(2025).md
[5] Database-Engine-Storage-(2026).md
